dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: How fares the hunt this time around, OK? I bet it could be better, so, erm, here! Maybe this'll help.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Let's take a little hunting expedition, yeah? You'll do most of the work, but hey, I'll be with you in spirit!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Sorry you're still stuck down there. Sometimes I feel stuck myself, but, a good hunt always sets me straight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: When I am on the hunt, my mind is clear, and nothing stands between me and my target. Though, have a blessing just in case you can't relate!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd love to know about your life down there. I'll try and help you live through it this time so that way you can tell me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: The wretches whom you face down there have died already, sometimes more than once. Should make for easy targets, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: I've never come across a target I couldn't take down. I expect the same of you, you hear me? Hopefully you hear me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Good hunting, Zagreus! The sooner you get out of there the sooner you can save me from my insufferable relatives. Please?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Zagreus, good luck out there, this time. I'd tell you 'may your aim be true' but I can guarantee it, actually.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: The hunt requires utter concentration, so, I'll leave you to it, and... just watch your every move in silence over here...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: You want to make it out of there, you... don't have to listen to a word I say, to be quite honest, but... my huntress blessings ought to help.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, Zagreus, you getting out of there this time, or am I going to have to keep on hunting by myself for the time being? Probably?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Looks to me like you could use a little help, so I'll just have a look in my big bag of blessings here, and, oh! Here's just the thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: The hunt comes down to kill-or-be-killed. Except for us immortals, who have to live with our shame, which is worse?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Those wretched things harassing you throughout that Underworld just won't give up, will they? Well, just keep killing them, I guess!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Need a hunting partner, Zagreus? Not that I'm able to join you myself down there, or even hear you respond for that matter, but, anyway, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus, it's me! I'm here to join the hunt with you again. Let's show them what we can do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Hello again, you ready for this hunt? Me, I've been looking forward since last time, so, let's get on with it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Stay on your guard, tread lightly, and all that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Keep a sharp eye out there, won't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Good hunting down there, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: What are you hunting this time, I wonder.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll be looking out for you. If you don't mind of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Remember, Zagreus, kill-or-be-killed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: How about I join you for this hunt, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, you take care of yourself, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Got you something I bet you could use.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: You know how long it took Callisto to become a master of the hunt? I'm sure you can still learn a thing or two!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: I was about to head out with Callisto for a hunt, though I can spare you a quick Boon, I guess!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: I have to meet Callisto soon, but it won't take me but a moment here to get you on your way!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Someday perhaps you'll join me and Callisto for a hunt! But as for now...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Greetings to you from the forest! Callisto and the woodland nymphs say hi!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: You'd make a real asset on the hunt, I think. Not that Callisto and I need assistance, though.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: The finer points of hunting are not easy to communicate, though you're learning from experience, at least!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: My senses sure are going to be sharp if I keep trying to keep up with you like this in all that dark down there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: A good, clean shot is all it takes, most of the time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: You getting anywhere down there yet, Zagreus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: What are you waiting for, let's get to hunting, then!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Not much of a hunt when your prey knows exactly where you are, but it'll do!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: I hope your every strike cuts to the vital bits of all your prey, there, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: I brought some hunting gear for you to try, if you could give me your attention, Zagreus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: All right, Zagreus, catch your breath, decide, and then what say we get you out of hell maybe?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: What are we hunting this time, Zagreus? Don't tell me wretched dead, again?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: You see how easily I tracked you down? Unless you went out of your way for me, that is?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: I have a Boon for you, here, Zagreus, though just be sure you don't embarrass me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, perfect timing, Zagreus, now stock up and head out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: You want to make it out of there, my Boons are just about the only way to go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't you let your father or let anybody push you around, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Bet I can help you break out of your father's realm in no time, Zagreus!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Decided I would pack you something extra for this hunt, if you can handle it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Enough toying around with our prey, there, Zagreus. What do you say we finish it, this time?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: I know it must be such a struggle for you, Zagreus, so here! In case this helps.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: A great hunter ought to have great tools, Zagreus, so here! Please allow me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Wish I could hunt with you myself, but here's the next best thing, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Have to say, I had a pretty decent time with everybody at our little visit recently! Met you, Uncle Hades, even great Achilles! Still don't quite appreciate you tricking us, but eh! I can get over it eventually. Anyway, you still could use assistance from me, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Living on Olympus here, I'm surrounded by my family, but I always felt alone. I know that's not the same as your experience, but, I'm just saying I relate to what you're going through.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Why would you side with...?! Ugh, why do I even bother with some lesser god!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: I thought we had an understanding, Zagreus. But, this wouldn't be the first time I was wrong about someone.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Huh, so that's your choice! Maybe you're more like my relatives than I'd care to admit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Good shot, there, Zagreus! I guess you got me. Now why don't you go take a shot at this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't be messing with my feelings, Zagreus. My loyalty's hard-won, and quickly lost.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: You really missed an easy shot, here, Zagreus. But I will not. You'll see.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, good! Just when I had almost forgotten what an arrow to the back felt like!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Am I some kind of pushover to you, Zagreus? Is that what you think?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, look, turns out it's time for target practice once again! Start running, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: What made you think I'd stand for this, from you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah-hah, I knew you'd pull this old maneuver, Zagreus. And I am ready for you, now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: You're getting quite predictable when you betray me like this, Zagreus! Start running, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh really, that's the way it's going to be? You Underworld gods are all alike!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Now listen, Zagreus, admittedly this is a little strange, even from me. I have a message for your bow! That's right, just go on up to Coronacht next time, and tell it with a firm, commanding voice, I see you drawn in the arms of the seventh avatar! Let me know how it goes, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, hold a moment, Zagreus, I could have sworn... you were so close, before, but now you're back? Ah, must have tripped up just when victory was close at hand, it happens all the time. We'll get you out of there, you'll see!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Let's see. You blundered headlong into danger for the past six chambers or more. And you made such a racket I could almost hear it from Olympus. I have to say, your hunting skills are rather unconventional.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm with you every step till you arrive here, Zagreus. Then I will probably ignore you like the rest. Just warning you ahead of time, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, I once heard Lord Hades has in his employ the greatest hero of the Trojan War, you happen to know anything about that? I'm kind of a fan. You see Achilles, tell him he's the best for me!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: So, I have heard that Achilles, himself, taught you how to fight! No wonder you can hold your own down there, that is amazing...! You be sure to honor him from time to time, got it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: That Fury down in Tartarus has been a real problem, hasn't she. I see the huntress instinct in her. She thinks she knows your weaknesses. Don't let yourself become predictable.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, uh, Zagreus! There's something I've been wondering, and don't take this the wrong way or anything, OK? Oh, no. It's her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Aphrodite_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, Artemis, you're absolutely tongue-tied! Due to our little godling in the Underworld, or a little due to me? Hm-hm, you shall figure it all out I'm sure!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Look, what was I, ugh, just, never mind. I'll talk to you some other time, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Erm, Zagreus, despite my hunter's instincts here, it seems I was unable to elude Aphrodite in this particular case, who insisted that we present ourselves to you at the same time, for whatever reason...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Aphrodite_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, come now, Artemis! I'm sure you're very capable of hiding from a common beast, but I can see you plain. Why not be honest with yourself, next time?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus, I wish we all could hear you so that good Lord Ares here might be persuaded that my powers are entirely for different purposes than his!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Ares_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps our skillsets once were meant for different purposes, dear Artemis; but who are we to doubt the end result? Observe what shall transpire once we grant our kin our combined might.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus, I was just hoping for a little privacy before this hunt, which is consistently my preference here, except, you see, Lord Ares always seems to come sniffing about, first sign of any hunting instruments!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Ares_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, my dear Artemis, the hunt is but a prelude to a war, is that not so? For if all mortalkind knew naught of hunting, why, they would not be nearly so adept at killing one another.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Look, Lord Ares, if I lend you better marksmanship ability, you think that you could bother someone else?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, hi there, Zagreus, I was just failing to persuade the Lady Demeter to ease up off the snowy stretch that we've been having for a while. Some of the mortals are quite weary of it now, and it sure doesn't make the hunting any easier!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The mortals are resourceful, Artemis. Their one redeeming quality, perhaps! They'll find a way through our long winter here. They ought to, since they brought it on themselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, great, it's you, Zagreus! Here, let me offer you some fine new Boons, as well as Lord Dionysus here, who I am sure would love to talk your ear off just as much as mine!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh, tells me I'm being awful loud, and to back off, all sorts of nasty stuff like that, what can you do, though, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: You know my father and my uncle, by now, right, Zagreus? I don't know which of them is worse. Uh, I mean... superior! Superior, despite technically spending most of his time in the seas, beneath Olympus, isn't that right, Uncle?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Poseidon_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Artemis, you needn't boast to little Hades there on my account! Besides! Whether you're in the sea, above it, or beneath, makes little difference to your measure as a god! You two are young as yet! Keep at it, and you'll soon be practically as great as I!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Tsk, um, Zagreus... rather than greet you as I normally would do, instead, allow me to present you my illustrious, kind, generous lord father, your greatest Uncle Zeus!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zeus_01
dialogueLines.text: Erm, thank you for the introduction, Daughter! Now, you run along, go frolic with your nymphs, or something! Leave me to assist my nephew, here. Bestow on him your faint blessing, if you must.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Cousin, you've met Aphrodite, no? She likes beguiling others with her charms; but her intentions with you I believe are pure. You'll find our strengths are complementary.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: My warlike brother Ares reached you, didn't he. I've always found his conduct quite disturbing. At least it seems he's helping you for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll tell you something, Cousin: If even Artemis is aiding you, it's proof your cause is just. She doesn't often take an interest in family affairs.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The biting cold surrounding you must mean the Goddess of Seasons decided to take an interest in your struggle, Zagreus? I did not count on Demeter being a part of this. Take heart, though use discretion around her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Did Dionysus truly visit you before? He tends to seek out revelry, not conflict. I'm pleased to know he's making himself useful here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus, perhaps it is a fancy of imagination, but it seems almost as though, this time, you bear the blessing of... your own father! If Lord Hades is assisting you in any way in this, then I shall hold out hope that your relationship yet has capacity to heal, to some extent at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: So you've another blessing from Lord Hades, then. Zagreus, I'm under no delusion you and your father shall become the closest friends, from here. Neverthless, I do believe it's possible to move forward even such a relationship as yours. Believe me... I know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You arrived sooner than anticipated, Cousin, and my estimations seldom are inaccurate. All for the best, of course. Perhaps you're simply faster than I thought.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You bear my Owl Pendant, noble Cousin! Wherever it should go, I faintly see its shimmer from afar. Even where you reside! For owls see in total darkness, after all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I have grown closer than expected with the goddess Nyx in all of this, Cousin. It is quite evident to me how much she cares for you. I must admit I envy your relationship, sometimes. For I've made do without a mother's presence in my life.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Lord Ares has suggested he and goddess Nyx have grown much closer to each other, in all this. Nothing untoward, if you must know. Ares and I have often disagreed, about so many things, and yet we share a fondness for the Night herself, it seems.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Sometimes I think about the goddess Nyx, and how I long for her to be the mother that I never had, Cousin. Is it improper that I say such things to you? One would expect I would already have somebody on Olympus, yet... it often feels as though I am the mother, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: This is it, then. In the name of Hades! Olympus, this is an official message! Lady Athena, I trust you'll quickly figure this one out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Is this some kind of offering for me? No, hold a moment, this appears to be a message! Part of one. Then, can this mean...? I have to notify the others. I trust they also have received something from you, or shortly shall.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You've met the master of the seas, Poseidon, haven't you? Our uncle's affable but quick to anger. Be on your guard with him, is my advice.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The goddess Nyx has indicated you possess a list of prophecies from the Three Fates. I've such a list upon Olympus, here! It seems to be connected to your own, as several prophecies already are fulfilled. Isn't it such an honor to urge Fate along?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I understand you've been in contact with my father. Your uncle Zeus, I mean. He's taken interest in your plight; you should feel honored!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The one called Nyx... she is your mother, as I understand? We Olympians rarely consort with the chthonic gods. Though Nyx herself reached out to me about your plight, and I was spurred to act.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Please send the goddess Nyx my kind regards. That she supports you in your effort to escape her own domain suggests a selflessness I would not have expected.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: It's such a cruelty that your father hid the knowledge of your birth and life from us, my cousin. It's unacceptable.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father Hades and my father Zeus have not communicated in some time, and Zeus refuses to explain. Something transpired between the two of them. Perhaps you know better than I.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: There's something that I've wished to tell you, Zagreus: There is no shame in your upbringing. Many of us on Olympus, we likewise were born and raised under the strangest circumstances. Someday I'll have to tell you about mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit_Intro
dialogueLines.text: It's got to be her. Then, here goes nothing... Ahem. In the name of Hades! Olympus, I accept this message.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Hail, noble Cousin. Now, let's get you from that miserable place. I'll see that all of us upon Olympus do our part, beginning here with me.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm indebted to you, Goddess, for all that you and your side of the family have done for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Well it simply isn't in my nature to allow such offerings to go unanswered, noble Cousin. In turn, I wish for you to have this, with my compliments.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: For you, Lady Athena, without whom I would assuredly be lost.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: This is an unexpected gesture, Cousin! I do appreciate it, though please do not feel obliged. I offer you my friendship, not a loan to be repaid.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I have a simple token for you, Goddess. Although it's insufficient thanks for all the aid you've given me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You are most gracious, Cousin, to extend this offering. You honor me, yet I would ask you think of your well-being for the time, not mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Goddess Athena, please accept this gift. Your wisdom ever guides me toward the light.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: My, how very generous of you, to offer me this gift! I'm very grateful, though I ask that you not go out of your way on my account. Let's please stay focused, here?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: As I remain committed to my task, Goddess, I'm reminded none of this would have been possible without your aid. And so, I must give thanks, even if through a meager offering.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I know there isn't much that you can do to show your gratitude at present, Zagreus. But I would ask that you save it for when your task is complete. You've more important matters to attend to now!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: As I remain committed to my task, Goddess, I'm reminded none of this would have been possible without your aid. And so, I must give thanks, even if through a meager offering.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I know there isn't much that you can do to show your gratitude at present, Zagreus. But I would ask that you save it for a better time. You've more important matters to attend to, now!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Lady Athena, I know there's more at stake for you in this than just my plight... you care so much about this family. That care and your determination spurs me on. I'm ever grateful.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps there really is no time quite like the present when it comes to showing thanks to one another, my dear Cousin. You've opened my eyes to the possibility, though I remain steadfast in my resolve to fight for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I know this Ambrosia originated on Olympus, Lady Athena. Though through its long voyage to these depths, I trust its savor is more worthy of your grace. No one's ever had a greater cousin. And I'll never forget what you've done for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus? When Nyx revealed herself to me and I first learned of you, I saw it as an opportunity to bridge the great divide between Olympus and your father's realm. But, having had all these glimpses into who you are, it is an even greater honor to have forged this partnership with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You brandish the Eternal Spear we call Varatha...! So then it wasn't lost during the war. A magnificent weapon, in deserving hands.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, you wield Coronacht the Heart-Seeker...! I never took you for an archer, though the power of that bow is undeniable. Use it wisely!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You bear Aegis, the original itself...! My own shield was forged much later, in its image. You'll have to tell me someday how you found it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You got your hands on Malphon, the Twin Fists, I see. No ordinary weapon. Prone to fits of savagery quite unbecoming of a well-trained warrior. Then watch yourself, and throw not caution to the wind.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The Rail of Adamant, itself, has found its way into your hands, Cousin? A malignant thing, if you were to ask me, and not one I would ever use, myself. Unless, perhaps, I had no other choice. I suppose it must have been that way for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: For you, my cousin Zagreus, I wish to offer the most potent of the blessings I am able to bestow. Make wise your use of this, and rise toward us!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The circumstances spur me now to offer you the greatest of my powers, Cousin. Take my strength, and strike the darkness down!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: It feels unseemly to make contact from that most-unpleasant boatman's stash of ill-gotten wares. Nonetheless, this was an opportunity.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You're difficult to track there in the depths, Cousin; though, the boatman Charon thankfully is proving of some help.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The river-boatman Charon is beholden to your father's realm, but seems agreeable enough, for a fair price.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: A shrewd expenditure of wealth is sometimes all that stands between a victory and sore defeat, isn't that right, Cousin?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: We cannot effectively communicate with Charon from Olympus, but he is amenable to certain trades, and seems unfettered by your father's influence.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You're badly injured aren't you, Cousin? I wish that I could will the pain away, though at the least I can provide you this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Look at you, Zagreus, you're barely fit to fight from all the injury that you've sustained! I'll try to keep you going for as long as possible.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: It seems to me that you've sustained life-draining injuries already, Zagreus. But don't lose hope. We can sustain you in a proper fighting shape.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You may have suffered many wounds but shall not perish imminently, Cousin. Not if I have anything to say about it. Here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Zagreus... your recent battles evidently haven't gone as hoped. Nevertheless, always strive onward, while you still draw breath.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, let's continue on, already, Zagreus. There isn't any point in feuding, and there's much to do before your quest is done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Now then! I hope we can move past this little incident, and keep our focus on the task at hand.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm satisfied we understand each other better, now, don't we. What do you say we carry on, and get you from this place?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: All is forgiven, Zagreus, and I take no offense. Exacting vengeance is just something we Olympians all do from time to time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd say I've bickered with you quite enough for now, Cousin. Let us continue down a more productive path.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: We've better things to do, I think, than fight amongst ourselves over a matter such as this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I would very much prefer to think we both know better than to let old grudges stew forever, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You see I have my father's temper, Zagreus. I am able to keep it in check, though I expect you to do your part.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Conflict between the members of our family is inevitable, Zagreus. All we can do is learn and grow from it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I see no benefit to staying angry, Cousin. I promised Nyx that I would help rather than hinder you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father also made some questionable choices, Zagreus. But all of us are family, after all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Supporting you through your ordeal, Cousin? It's reminiscent of the thrill of doing battle by the side of those whom I hold dear. I may be safe upon our mountaintop, though I am with you there, as well... for every well-earned victory, and bitter loss.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Keep fighting, Cousin. Fight from out of that eternal gloom. I trust that you'll be joining us upon Olympus shortly.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I have your back, Cousin. Rise now to your rightful station with my blessings!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: May the light and fury of Olympus guide you from the dark, Cousin. I've a token of it for you here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Push forward, noble Cousin. I shall be keen to see you circumvent the perils that the Fates arranged for you this time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Vanquish all who stand against you, noble Cousin. I and your relatives upon Olympus shall assist you in so doing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Sometimes I think about what you've endured, to have been living in that loathsome squalor, Cousin. I swear, you'll have your justice.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Do not fear your father's cruel legions, Cousin! Their lives are long since forfeit, and they cannot stand against one of your birthright, nor against my power.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Yours is a brazen plan of action, Zagreus, and I admire it. The boldest strategy often prevails. Especially with my support.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Take heart, Cousin; for you have found me even in the suffocating darkness of the Underworld. May my power be of some assistance.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Your struggles have not gone unnoticed, Cousin, so take heart. Eternity awaits you once you finally break free.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Here on Olympus is the opposite of everything about that lifeless squalor you reside in, Zagreus. Fight on and come to us!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Cousin, I grow angry merely thinking of your situation. May what power I can offer be of service as you struggle towards your fate.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father chose to sever ties with all of us, and stew alone within his land beneath the earth. But you are not beholden to his actions. Fight!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Your labors make you stronger, noble Cousin. And, the blessings I now offer ought to make you stronger still.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I've found you once again, dear Cousin, and my strength is yours; so much of it as I can send there to the bowels of the earth.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm here again to lend to you my power, noble Cousin. Though your own strength of will is how you'll finally break free of that cruel place.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm pleased to see your father's stubbornness is manifest in you as such determination, Zagreus. My blessing be with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: What wretched fools dare stand against you, Cousin? Punish them, so that they learn something they ought have learned in life.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Hold fast to your resolve, Cousin, and climb on toward the surface. No matter how often your foothold falters, I am here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: May wisdom and good fortune guide you toward the light of day this time, Cousin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall not keep you, Zagreus, except to offer you some of my power to improve your chances of escape. Now quickly choose, and go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The Underworld's wretched dead shall fear you yet, Cousin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Strength and wisdom guide you from the darkness, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The low cunning of your lifeless enemies is not a match for your divine might.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Remember I am always watching over you, dear Cousin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You'll have your justice in due course, Cousin. I promise you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The might and fury of Olympus courses through you, noble Cousin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I take it you've a plan in mind this time for how you're going to escape, don't you, Cousin?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall not keep you, Zagreus, except to offer you my power to improve your chances of escape.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Remain relentless in your effort, Zagreus. For perseverance is a quality we all possess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I and your family on Olympus all stand ready to support you in your efforts, noble Cousin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Remember, doubt and hesitancy are your real enemies, Cousin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I know that you are more than capable even without my power, Cousin. But just in case...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Envision your success, and choose the means by which you shall achieve it, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall not offer empty words, dear Cousin; I shall instead offer you this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: With our ferocity combined, Cousin, what chances do your father's forces even have?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: My support alone is not enough to see you through this, Zagreus; but it certainly shall help.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Your foes shall soon find it impossible to overcome your strength, combined with mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I've every confidence that you shall imminently put my blessing to good use.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Let not your sense of purpose waver, Zagreus. Visualize the outcome you desire.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Make not the same mistake twice, and you shall always grow wiser, no matter what else.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall do everything within my power to defend you from the perils of your journey, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Take heed against your father's legions, Zagreus. And take also my power.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Do not forget that changing strategy can be the key to victory, Cousin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I cannot glean the details of your predicament right now, Cousin. Though maybe this shall help.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The might and honor of Olympus shield you against the darkness and the dead, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I take it having an impenetrable defense against your enemies may be of some use, Cousin?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm not in any mood to trifle with your father's nonsense this time, Zagreus. So, use this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: These are among the mightest defenses I can offer, Zagreus. Choose wisely, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I've readied several of my most potent blessings so your victory can be achieved, Cousin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Observe, dear Cousin, for this time I offer you uncommonly superior protection.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: It was my privilege to have visited your father's House, and briefly had a chance to meet you, Zagreus. Nyx and your mother each are very shrewd; perhaps you've taken after both of them. As for your new responsibilities... exposing weak points in defenses is my specialty.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: It's fortunate your troubles with your mother were resolved. Persephone, I mean! To think I led all of my family to believe your mother was the goddess Nyx, instead. It seems I was an unwitting accomplice to a bit of a deception, there. But Nyx, I know she merely tried to help.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You're nothing like your father, Zagreus. I mean that as a compliment. He cut himself off from the rest of us. Many times have we attempted peace, to no avail.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I so look forward to our meeting face to face amongst the gods, dear Cousin. Olympus shall be merrier and wiser once you're here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Hail and good tidings to you, noble Cousin. May you be swift and thorough vanquishing the dead who dare oppose you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Remain relentless in your effort to escape, dear Cousin. In spite of all our differences, perseverance is a quality we all possess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You can always count upon assistance from me, Zagreus. I always shall prioritize the needs of our family above everything else.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Let my voice be a glimmer in the darkness, noble Cousin; though, may the blessing I am about to provide be even more than that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I thought we were on better terms, Cousin. I ask only for your cooperation, yet still you turn your back...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm disappointed you would spurn me like this, Cousin. Know that I retaliate not out of spite, but by necessity.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You'd choose one of my relatives, instead of me...? Though I myself was first to contact you? Alas, I must respond to you in kind.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Upon Olympus, I'm your closest confidant. You'd best remember this before you spurn me again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: We Olympians, we are a prideful family, Cousin. You cannot simply choose a favorite without spurring one of us to wrath.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I understand you've made your choice, Cousin. And I, in turn, now have my family's reputation to uphold.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: If you had given due consideration to this matter, then I doubt you would have chosen this outcome.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: No one in our family can avoid taking sides forever, Zagreus. But you can take the more sensible side, at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: A senseless, punishable choice. But if you wish to face the consequences, so be it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You could have avoided this outcome any number of ways, yet still you risk incurring my wrath.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Are you testing the limits of my patience with you, Zagreus? Then, I shall test your limits, in turn.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I knew there was a possibility that you would side against me once again, but your doing so never ceases to amaze, Cousin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: If this is your idea of humor, Zagreus, then I shall make it clear to you again that I am unamused.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I think we understand by now that when you test my patience, I shall test your ability to survive.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Were you not close to having reached the surface lately, Cousin? Then, this can only mean some unforeseen impediment prevented your escape.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: There must be some unconquerable force or foe awaiting you, just at the border of your realm and ours. We cannot even see it through a shroud of darkness, there. I shall make certain that your family knows it may take considerable time to surpass... so fear not, for we are patient, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Despite whatever difficulties you've encountered, dear Cousin, again and again, you have never yielded. I must admit, your strength of will is quite inspiring. For, even I have doubts, from time to time. It's all the clearer to me why the goddess Nyx spoke so highly of you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: It comforts me to see how far you've come, Cousin. And I am confident that you shall make your way, eventually. I shall look forward to our conversations then.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You reached the flames of Asphodel, well done! You may have quite a distance still to go, but having crossed through Tartarus is no small step, Cousin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I see you've made it all the way to hallowed Elysium, Cousin? A significant way toward the surface, and if you could get there the one time, surely you have what it takes to do it again, and overcome the dangers that await therein.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I understand you nearly reached the surface lately, Zagreus. My confidence remains unshaken that you shall get out of there, eventually. No matter what sorts of unknown horrors lie shrouded beyond the boundary there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: You've met your distant relatives by now. We don't all share the same demeanor, nor see eye to eye. Though, all of us, I think, wish you the best.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Your predicament has pulled our family together, united in a desire to assist you, Cousin. Even those who typically do not think much of those other than themselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Aphrodite_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, if only I had wisdom such as yours, Athena dear, so that I was more capable of picking up on subtle jabs and insults such as that! You're not fooling anybody with your feigned benevolence, you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: The world is not all lies and deceit as you make it out to be, Aphrodite. Sometimes, a lending of assistance is no more than it appears to be. Now either back up Zagreus, or stand aside.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: If you've a moment, Zagreus? One of my relatives has claimed that I usurp your time. So I invited her to contact you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Um, hello, Zagreus... Athena here is grandstanding or trying to prove a point, or something. I don't know. Anyway, nice talking to you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, I suppose that's settled, then. I am afraid such interactions aren't uncommon here amongst my family; but what's a family without flaws?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: I bring to you the Lady Demeter, Cousin. I've asked that she please rest assured knowing we have your matter well under control. Though, in her infinite compassion, she has taken up your cause, nevertheless!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you needn't lavish me with your faint praise, Athena dear. In all your boundless intellect, I'd have expected you would know I see through your intentions, plain as day.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Some members of our family do not yet fully understand just how precarious your situation is, Cousin. My Lord Dionysus here, he goes about as though this is a festive matter of some sort!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, come on, Athena girl, you really mean to tell me that discovering a long-lost relative isn't a cause for celebrating right away, I mean, you can't be telling me a thing like that? Because if Zag isn't a cause for feasting, I don't know what is!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: When I first notified Lord Father Zeus and other members of our family of your existence, Zagreus, it was Uncle Poseidon here who first leapt up, and pledged his power over sea and earth to you. Is that not so, Uncle?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Poseidon_01
dialogueLines.text: Exactly so, Athena! You, with that fine memory of yours! For it isn't everyday I learn my doleful brother Hades sired a fine son! He does not know how good he has it, there! And you, Nephew, could have it even better, here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Noble cousin, my lord father has taken keen interest in your journey and your plight, and now seeks to aid you with the thunder of heaven itself. And you've my support, as well, of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zeus_01
dialogueLines.text: I thank you for the generous introduction, fair Athena, and I always rest assured when you are close at hand. I trust with our combined might, our good Zagreus is virtually certain to succeed!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: The charms of goddess Aphrodite are upon you unmistakably, although I shall be curious to see if her command over one's sense of love shall have any effect beneath the earth.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: It is not unexpected that you consorted with the violent Ares, and now bear his Boon. He still worships me, you know; yet, I would say he does not know me very well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I understand the hunting-goddess Artemis recently has turned her gaze towards you. It is unexpected she would offer her assistance, even to a relative in need.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: This wise goddess Athena has imbued you with her charms, I see, O Son of Hades. Perhaps to her you are a tool with which her broken family may as yet be mended.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: You have not yet used the waking-phrase that I imparted? Or perhaps you merely chose not to present me the result. I would quite like to see it, at some point. Remember, Son of Hades: Say to the all-seeing artifact, I see you stand your ground against the serpent's flame.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I sense that you have not yet spoken with the all-seeing artifact you call Aegis. Tamper with the Fates' design, then, all you like. But if you change your mind, do speak the words to it: I see you stand your ground against the serpent's flame.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Out of the infinitely spanning possibilities, here before me stands the Son of Hades, bearing my all-seeing artifact, appearing in the aspect of a bearer yet unborn, who shall be known throughout the north as Beowulf. How very droll.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: You have arrived within a certain shroud of darkness, Son of Hades. The goddess Nyx's ancient shawl. I had not sensed its void in a considerable amount of time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: When you expire, and then suddenly return to life within your father's residence, perhaps it is akin to when I first gained consciousness so long ago. Perhaps I, too, had just expired, only to reawaken.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Embittered Demeter has reached you, hasn't she? She once was full of warmth, you know; but lately, as I understand, she is more prone to taking it away.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: The drunkard Dionysus offered you his services, I see? He can be rather unpredictable, and therefore offers me a source of some amusement.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: You may have wondered, Son of Hades, how it is you came to find me in this space, when I have gone unseen by most for quite some time. I have wondered this, as well, and think that our connection to Goddess Nyx is the most likely answer.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: How strange it is to be subjected to the Fates. But do not fall into the cognitive delusion that they have already made your choices for you, Son of Hades. Rather, they are keen about anticipating every choice that everyone shall make. Such as the following.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I am impressed that you were able to procure a specimen out from the flowing currents here in my domain, with nothing but a simple instrument. I shall look forward to your subsequent attempts.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father and his brothers, when they sealed the Titans in the darkest reaches of this world, that was the last they saw of me, as well. You are dissimilar from them, and I feel safe communicating thus with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Swift Hermes spurs you on, I see, O Son of Hades. Beware the narrow distance between hastiness and swiftness; a distinction I am not so sure the god of travel fully understands.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I sense your consternation, Son of Hades, as you look about this space; you have another question, and are free to ask.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, it's just... I sometimes wonder, Master Chaos... if you created all of this, then... who created you? What was this place before you arrived?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: All of this simply came to be, and so did I, presumably after a former time had finally reached its end. I hesitate to say it was an act of will. It was more like a sudden shift, as when a conflagration happens from a spark. I arrived at such a time, and all else then arrived, from me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I trust the small round catalyst which I provided you has been of value, Son of Hades? It is my hope it makes your journeys to this space less difficult, perhaps inducing them on a more frequent interval.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I understand that Zeus and all his kin on Mount Olympus think that you are born of Hades and the goddess Nyx? I was amused to learn of this deception. Nyx has mothered many of the Underworld's own, so, why not you?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: She isn't my birthmother, no, but she is like a mother to me. Do you know her?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I know the goddess Nyx quite well, of course. Though, it has been a while since we last communicated, and I fear... well, I was not the charitable sort, back then. It is my hope that Nyx is faring well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I knew you would arrive. But only in the moments prior to the fact. Thus, I am experiencing residual surprise. Your movements through the Underworld are as though a veil of darkness enshrouds you. The goddess Nyx's influence, perhaps? Respond.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I... do not know how to respond to that, exactly, Master Chaos, but, Nyx, yes, she has been aiding me, in my attempts to flee my father's realm.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: You move in darkness, not as infinitely black as hers, but reminiscent to me, nonetheless. I would invite her to try to elude me, and then attempt to locate her. A minor amusement for the both of us. Now, I have you for this, I see.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I was at times uncharitable with the goddess Nyx, when we were closer, long ago. After she bore her first children, I nevertheless expected her attention to remain undivided. I kept creating. When finally I slowed, I realized my mistake. But our connection had been severed, by that point.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I sense that something is the matter, Son of Hades. Speak, then, what is on your mind? I can decipher some of it without your telling though would rather hear it from you voluntarily.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm, Master Chaos? The goddess Nyx, she... seems to want to speak with you again. I wonder, would you maybe contact her sometime? She seems convinced it is your move to make if you so choose.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: The goddess Nyx wishes to hear from me...? Now that is unexpected, Son of Hades, and I wonder if, perhaps, this is a jest of yours. Though, your intentions evidently are sincere. Then, I shall make no promises in this, except that... I am considering the possibility.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Son of Hades, should eventually you spawn your own offspring... I recommend maintaining sub-infinite distances with them in space and time. Even if they are most tiresome, there always seems to come a point when one wishes to be in their presence once again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: After some consideration, and on your recommendation, I attempted lately to make contact with the goddess Nyx again. She is quite distant from me now, of course. If you happen to know... did she receive the message well? Respond.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, Master Chaos, she can be a little difficult to read at times, but... I got the impression she was quite surprised, even excited to have heard from you again. She said that she would think about how to respond. Or, whether to respond.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I see. Well, she shall find a way, if it is warranted. I always did expect the world from her. There were times when I expected her to shine as bright as day, though she is Night. When she achieved a certain level of ability... she left. And I could not find her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I have detected some unusual activity within the reaches of the Underworld, Son of Hades... vast sums of Darkness, collected in an ancient catalyst, which I see now almost like a beacon, shining in the night. This either is the work of Nyx, or you, or both. I am anticipating the result.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: It pleases me immensely, Son of Hades, to have seen the goddess Nyx again. The significant amount of time that has elapsed since last this happened dulled sufficiently old pains, I think. Thus, it becomes a basis on which our connection may, in time, take shape again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Again I sensed some sort of shifting in your father's residence. You seek to ask something of me about it, do you not? Respond.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I'm glad you asked, it's just... my mother, she's... we're reaching out to the Olympians. Trying to reconnect the family. It's been a while, relatively, since she and Father saw the others, but... in case a gathering occurs, I did wish you to know you're cordially invited, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Cordially invited...? A generous proposal, Son of Hades, though unless your gathering is to occur within my space, please understand it would be difficult for me to bring the full entirety of my presence there. However... I think I can arrange to be with you in some capacity. Thank you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: You have met many of the Olympians by now, each spurring you toward their mountaintop. They place such value on familial bonds, yet none of them remember who I am. Do not place too much faith in them, O Son of Hades.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I ask you listen but not speak, O Son of Hades, whilst the Olympians? They do not ask at all. They merely send their messages to you, unprompted, providing a choice of blessing, but no say. I seek to understand their point of view. But it is difficult.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: When you extend an offering to the Olympians, for a fleeting instant, it appears as though they are able to hear your prayer. Or so I have observed. How interesting that your thoughts are capable of breaking free, yet you cannot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Son of Hades, I believe I have deduced something I ought have sooner recognized. Your mother must have been one who called herself Persephone. Her stay within the Underworld was brief, from my perspective, but impactful, nonetheless. You seek her now? Respond.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I do. She's somewhere on the surface. That's why I keep on finding you like this. I'm trying to find her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I hope that your attempts achieve success. She is beyond my senses, presently, though of course I am still able to assist you in your journey, thus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Son of Hades, I believe I have deduced something I ought have sooner recognized. Your mother must have been one who called herself Persephone. Her stay within the Underworld was brief, from my perspective, but impactful, nonetheless. You seek her now? Respond.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Master Chaos... you should know I have located her, already. Though where she is, I cannot stay for long. So I am visiting, as often as I can. It's... not unlike our visits here. Only, somehow, even harder to get to where she is.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I see. Then, it is good to know that you have been in contact. And I trust that your successive visits shall be worth their while. Not unlike our visits here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Son of Hades, if you locate the one who called herself Persephone, shall it then come to pass that we no longer see each other in this way? Respond.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh! I... Master Chaos, I don't know what to expect from there. But, to be direct with you, if I could leave the Underworld forever... I think I would. Though, I would miss you. Many others here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I would miss you as well. Thank you for telling me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Lord Poseidon offered you his blessing, it would seem. He can be rather boastful and belligerent, exhibiting the most unattractive manners of your father and Lord Zeus alike.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: As we become acquainted, Son of Hades, I shall make myself quite clear in one respect: I fear I have a lack of patience for discussion. When visiting, please merely listen, then accept my mark, and then depart. Respond only when necessary. Or when prompted. Yes?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...Merely listen, so it's just like when I get those messages from the Olympians. Wait, you did just prompt me, Master Chaos, didn't you? Or should I have remained silent just now?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I cannot offer you advice. But you were prompted, yes. You may speak freely, then, or anytime, despite my wishes that you not do so. Absolute silence is my general preference. It may not be yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: You come to my domain bearing an all-seeing artifact that sprang from me after the dawn of time. It is considered a defensive instrument, I do believe. Though, I suspect you have discovered, or soon shall discover, it is nothing of the sort.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: You have traveled recently beyond my senses, Son of Hades. I persist here only in the fathomless depths. I know what lies out there, having created it. But I know nothing of what it is like, right now. Do not tell me. I prefer to think of all the possibilities.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I expected your arrival, Son of Hades. For I am able to see through you. Rather, through the all-seeing artifact, which sprang from me after the dawn of time, and now resembles, in your hands, its foremost form. Respond.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What, you must mean the Shield of Chaos, here? Your shield, Master Chaos, I should say. It's a remarkable artifact. Imposing, too! Your original creations must have been astounding.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Your response is acceptable. And I am grateful to have had my recent firsthand glimpses of this vast domain.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: You bear the blessing of the great Lord Zeus. He still believes he holds dominion over both heaven and earth. I think, perhaps, he has forgotten about me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, you have that all-seeing artifact of mine, but in the old guise that the great Lord Zeus once used when he retaliated violently against his parentage. I watched his movements through it for some time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Such darkness emanates from you, O Son of Hades, that I once again did not entirely anticipate you would arrive just now. I suspect even your father must have difficulty keeping track of you through the domain he calls his own.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: You move in darkness, practically unseen, Son of Hades. It is quite difficult even for me to sense your presence at times. The Olympians catch their glimpses now and then. But you are always in a shroud. Thus is the influence of Nyx.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: This is not someone I recognize... so, in the name of Hades! Erm, hello? May I ask who's calling?

dialogueLines.speaker: MysteryUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: ...The Olympians have all grown soft... would you not agree?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm... have we met?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, we have met, in a way. I know everybody, here, and there. You wish to leave this place? Then, it is my wish to make your doing so a little bit more interesting... and, if you encounter the Olympians someday, do give them my regards.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Master Chaos? Everyone makes offerings to my relatives upon Olympus. I wish to make an offering to you. Thank you for... everything.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: How droll of you to make attempts to tempt me with material things. Here, then, please in turn allow me this attempt at reciprocity.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: This is for you, Master Chaos. Everyone beneath the heavens and earth enjoys this stuff.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: You honor me in making such an offering, though please; it is not necessary to endear yourself using material means, for it is the thought alone which counts.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Master Chaos, forebear of the gods, it is an honor to have met you. Please, accept this gift.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Seldom do mortals or immortals think of making offerings to me. As I do not expect such token gestures, I am very much amused, and I accept.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Master Chaos, I pledge never to forget you or your good graces. May your wisdom and your whim forever guide me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Is this an offering of fealty or friendship, Son of Hades? I do not wish to know from you the answer, so that I might dwell on it and all the possibilities.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm ever-grateful that my journeys through the Underworld mean that we can get to know each other, Master Chaos.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Your repeated generosity with me is starting to become a bit predictable, and yet... somehow, I nonetheless appreciate it, still. How very odd.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Master Chaos, it's little wonder all existence sprang from you, because I see the noble qualities in so many I've met embodied in you. Please, accept this gift.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: If it is unclear yet, Son of Hades, I shall not be giving more to you in exchange for your repeated offerings. You give them to me knowing it shall serve no purpose and achieve no end. I know that there are some among both mortals and your kind for whom this is quite unacceptable.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: This is the greatest delicacy of Olympus, Master Chaos. It's for you. I think normally it would be difficult for the Olympians to get it to you here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: You make an even greater offering to me, knowing it shall produce no material result. You are aware that I am not persuadable? Yours is a squandering of resources and time. Although, of course, we do have each of these in great supply. Now then, respond.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...I just think the act of giving isn't quite the same as an exchange. I know I won't be getting any sort of other talisman or something in return. But, through these offerings, I can express my thanks. That's worth it by itself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I see. So then, the action is akin to theft. For you are getting something in return that I did not intend to give. I still have much to learn from my creations, Son of Hades. So let us say that we have made an even trade. My learning for your worth.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: If you'll forgive me, Master Chaos, this Ambrosia is intended to be given to those whom I care about, and I can think of few recipients I'd like to have it more. Please.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall accept this, as a final offering, O Son of Hades. For I have given it additional consideration, and decided that my previous analysis of your intentions was too narrow, too inaccurate. And, I regret comparing what you have been doing to an act of theft. Respond?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Don't even mention it, please, Master Chaos. All of this is new for me, as well. I try never to overanalyze... my father does enough of that for all of us. I just know, when I encounter the primordial creator, I am going to give thanks. For everything.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I see. Then, your response is quite acceptable. And, since the act of giving is not an exchange, let us say that you already had my gratitude.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps you did not think that I would seek you out among the boatman Charon's trifles, which precisely is the reason I have done it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: The boatman Charon understands my whims, and his loyalty is purchased easily by one for whom base wealth is meaningless, so, I am here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: It seems to me that you are very close to death, O Son of Hades, and yet you deigned to visit me within my space. I could hasten your demise, if you so choose?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I almost did not notice you, O Son of Hades, for you are closer now to death than life. Perhaps, though, we can stave off the inevitable for a little while.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Your deaths are but a temporary setback, Son of Hades. Though it appears to me you are quite close to having such a setback, now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Your next experience with death seems to be imminent, though let us see if I can hasten or forestall it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: It may seem cruel that I would wish you death, O Son of Hades. But from my perspective, it means you are much more likely to return to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Although you may succumb soon to your injuries, please make the most of your remaining life, rather than anticipate the next. The future is uncertain to all save the Fates.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I sensed a certain rumbling here within the bowels of the earth. It is as though it had ingested something that did not sit well... perhaps the earth itself wishes to spit you up and out...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: The Olympians are vying for your favor, are they not? So often they forget themselves. They force their blessings onto you, whilst I? I merely offer you a choice.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Your path is yours to shape as you see fit, regardless of the Fates' design. Together we may twist that path a bit, if you would like.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I both expected you to come and am surprised to see you, Son of Hades. Where shall your journey take you from this point?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Please tell me how my power over all existence may be of small assistance to you this time, Son of Hades?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: The tremors underneath the earth foretold of your arrival at this very moment, Son of Hades; and, I have anticipated it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: You chose to visit me again within these darkest reaches of the earth, as I, in turn, now choose to offer you the means to alter your reality.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Back when I conceived the world and the immortals, Son of Hades, I did not expect you would be standing here before me now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: You have arrived at last, just as I wondered how we might best alter one another's courses of existence here today.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Son of Hades, let me welcome your return here to my space within the Underworld, and bid you choose an alteration to the fabric of reality.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: It pleases me that you are here again; for, through you I can tinker with reality, along the fringes that are not yet fully formed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Your presence never ceases to intrigue me, Son of Hades; for you are not like the Olympians on high, who have forgotten me, or thusly choose to act.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: From my perspective I have summoned you, whilst you believe you came of your volition; both statements can be true, is that not so?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Come forth, O Son of Hades, and together, let us sow my namesake through the depths of what your father thinks his own domain.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: How fares these days the never-ending cycle of rebirth and death for you, O Son of Hades? Sufficiently amusing, I should hope?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Out of the infinitely spanning possibilities before us, Son of Hades, I have arbitrarily selected only three again from which to let you choose.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: The Fates have much in store for you, O Son of Hades. Perhaps, however, we can tamper with their weavings here to some extent.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Let us conspire to expand and frustrate your lord father's sense of both what is and is not possible within his Underworld.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Infinite possibilities can be tedious to choose from, so, let me provide you with a significantly smaller set with which you may yet change the Fates' designs.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Through the expanse of time, I often wondered, is it necessary to be fair? The immortals are my offspring; surely I should treat them equally. And yet I find that there is little sport in doing so.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Once more you have returned to my domain.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I have a proposition, Son of Hades.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I bid you welcome here within my space.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: How fares the solitary Son of Hades?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: What shall we do for one another, Son of Hades?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: You are within my presence, Son of Hades.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: What brings you here to me this time around?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Your such visits are agreeable with me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: How may the force of Chaos alter you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: You have arrived before me once again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I see that you are here, and so am I.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: You stand before me, Son of Hades.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: How might I mend the fabric of existence for you, Son of Hades?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: There need not be a reason for your visit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: How may I shape reality for you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: You are always welcome here within this space.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: What occupies your mind at this occasion?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Let us consider certain possibilities.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Among infinite outcomes, I propose these three.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Choose how to circumvent the Fates' design.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hail, Son of Hades.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I am amused to see you once again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: You have descended to my space once more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: You have returned to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: You are most welcome here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Among infinite possibilities, consider only these.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Choose carefully, in general, and now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: We are the sum of all decisions that we make, and that are made for us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: This space grows far more interesting when you occupy a small amount of it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: How you evaluate decisions is of limitless interest to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: The burden of the passing time shall force a choice from you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Is there not pleasure in determining an outcome, Son of Hades?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: My mark be with you, Son of Hades. You need merely choose its shape.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Though the waters of the Styx may wash away my mark, I can restore it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: The rhythm of your visits here over the course of time is pleasant, Son of Hades.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: You are already back, and you are always welcome, Son of Hades.
