dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Please visit me as frequently as you would like or dare.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I find myself anticipating your arrivals, Son of Hades.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I require no exchange of words with you. Merely a choice.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Lately, Son of Hades, I have sensed what I would call a sort of shift within your father's residence. Did something noteworthy occur, perhaps? Respond.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Master Chaos, it's Persephone, my mother. She's returned! She's chosen to return. So... things feel different, certainly, with her around, although... I didn't feel any sort of shift.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: You may not sense these shifts as I. The lightness of this space bends and adjusts with time. What you describe... it must have been the cause. I trust that the occasion is a welcome one. And I am pleased that you returned, nevertheless.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Tell me something, Son of Hades. If the one who called herself Persephone is back, then... why do you continue to return? Respond.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Master Chaos, it seems I've developed something of a talent around breaking out of here. Or, there, I mean, the Underworld, so... it's become more of an official role for me, I guess. If escape shouldn't be possible, then someone needs to find the gaps.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Indeed. Then, you shall visit with me still, despite accomplishing the principal object of your journey back when first we met. Good.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: It pleases me your gathering of the Olympians occurred. I must inform you... I had never once before detected quite so many of my offspring in a single space. At least not without violent intent. I appreciate that you included me, and that you have returned. Shall we continue, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: It pleases me your gathering of the Olympians occurred. I must inform you... I had never once before detected quite so many of my offspring in a single space. At least not without violent intent.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Have you grown trusting yet of the Olympians, for all their generosity? Perhaps you ought to let them know the truth about your journey's goal. They are vain, and think that it is them you seek.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'll never get out of here if not for their help, but... I can't let them know about Mother. She wants to remain hidden from them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: A difficult position to be in, Son of Hades. The Olympians may not appreciate being misled. But you can trust that I shall keep your secret. I wish to see how your predicament plays out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: My daughter, the goddess Nyx. When I learned the Olympians mistakenly assumed she was your mother, I did not fully consider the possibility that she essentially was a mother to you... the technicality of birth, aside. She cares for you to the extent a mother can.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Prior to your intervention, Son of Hades, I expected that the goddess Nyx had forgotten about me... whether purposefully, or from passage of time. I believed that to be forgotten was the one true inevitability. Perhaps, however, I was incorrect.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I find that it is difficult maintaining my connection with many consciousnesses, such as yours, and that of the goddess Nyx. Yet I am also finding that this difficulty is fully warranted. Our connections give me much to consider as I await our next exchange.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I bear a message for you, Son of Hades. One recorded in an age when certain concepts it describes did not yet all exist. Remember this: I see you stand your ground against the serpent's flame. A waking-phrase, to which you and the all-seeing artifact of my creation may respond.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: A waking-phrase... and an all-seeing artifact, you must mean Aegis, right? So I'm to say to it, I see you stand your ground against the serpent's flame, and it'll wake right up. Though, can you tell me what it means, or who?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I can tell you almost anything. However, I would much prefer it if you learned most of the answers you seek by yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, here is my favorite among those immortals dwelling underneath the Earth. How may I change your life today, O Son of Hades?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: May you enjoy a moment's peace within my space before venturing on in uncertainty. To my surprise, I must admit I do appreciate the company.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: From the rumblings of the earth, I have deduced your father, himself, stands against you when at last you are about to leave these depths. It must be quite an interaction between you. Have I deduced correctly? Respond.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: That's right, Master Chaos. My father personally takes it upon himself to stop me in the event none of his underlings beat him to it. He... can be a handful.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, then. Consider bringing to one of your future interactions the small round catalyst, which I provided you. The one in the shape you call an egg? It shall serve no purpose there at all. Yet if you succeed, then it shall bring us some amusement, shall it not?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I faintly sensed that you were able to confront your father while in possession of my small round catalyst. It seems my asking you, and your success, was in accordance with the Fates' design, as well. The result is more amusing even than expected. Thus, thank you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Your presence is appreciated, Son of Hades, but please leave us here, for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Forgive us, Son of Hades, as we are amid exchanging memories.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Son of Hades, I am pleased the goddess Nyx returned to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: Forgive me, Son of Hades, but I must continue this discussion with the goddess Nyx.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I had not seen the goddess Nyx in quite some time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: I am quite unaccustomed to having all this company of late.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: My daughter was just in the midst of telling me the latest occurrences about your House.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chaos_01
dialogueLines.text: As you are able to observe, the goddess Nyx and I are in the midst of conversation now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: All of the love and beauty in the world can wither in a season, Zagreus. Remember that when next the goddess Aphrodite makes her promises.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Lord Ares already got hold of you, has he? He's good at making mortals kill each other, yes. But he has utterly no knack for subtlety.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The gentle Artemis already found you, Zagreus? She takes my orders well; one cannot blame her, for she'd have nothing to hunt if not for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The heat of your surroundings must be difficult to bear, my little sprout. It's fortunate I've just the thing for that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Athena has imparted you her wisdom, Zagreus? A simulacrum of the real thing, at any rate. True wisdom only comes with age, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The mark of Chaos is upon you, isn't it? A force older than I! Yet one which you ought not trust so readily. No rhyme or reason to that one at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You stumbled into Dionysus, Zagreus? One of these days I shall prevent his vineyards all from ripening; put to the test his carefree attitude.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: We all are willful children, are we not? I'm daughter to Hyperion, you know; a Titan so obscure and hated that Lord Zeus, he started calling me his birth-sister, so much did he dislike this bit of truth! And to get a rise out of me, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: As seasons give to one another, so do generations, I suppose. Hyperion, my father? He'd not a one redeeming quality. My sisters and my foster-brothers and myself; this world owes us a debt for ridding it of him and his kind.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father Hades and I share a common fate, each having lost a child fully grown; except that he's a hollow fool, whose child left! Whereas my daughter never would have. Ever!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father always had a sternness to him that his brothers lacked. Thought him a decent sort, comparatively anyway; though evidently he is no such thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I have a brother, Helios, the Sun. A nonsense-talker with a flaming chariot. Why, he once told me that my Kore, that she went into that Underworld of yours! Preposterous. The clouds now blot him out entirely.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: This land is not unlike your own in certain ways. For, Helios the Sun has not shone down upon it for some time. How long, I really couldn't tell you, little sprout! Long enough for mortals to take notice, certainly.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The Frostbitten Horn which you bear once overflowed. It holds a certain power still, of course. But never again shall mortalkind take it for granted.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Our family, why, I think we understand something of loss, now, don't we, Zagreus? Unlike the mortals on the surface. Always begging me for plenty. They'll get none. But as for you, I have a plethora to offer!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You are entirely too young to have had meaningful experience with loss, young Zagreus. Pray to me that you never have to learn.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Should you achieve your goal, and see this surface-world, Zagreus, I've but one bit of knowledge to impart: Don't ever fall for mortals. Use them if you must, but do not waste your love on those who waste away.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Motherhood's a curse, young Zagreus. Be thankful you're immune. You sprang from Nyx herself, or so I hear? I sprang from Theia, whoever she was. And I once bore a child, too. Long gone. A mother's work... isn't it such a waste?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hope Mother knows what she's doing, in the name of Hades! Olympus, this is an official message! Lady Demeter, this is for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: What's all of this, young Zagreus, a message of some sort, for me, indeed? I... can't decipher it, although... it bears the marks of my two foster-brothers here, and... well! It seems we'd better compare notes!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The heat has risen quite considerably there, is that not so? Then if my foster-brother's tampered with the temperature, I'll have to cool things off.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: My foster-brother's wayward son appears! I, too, once lost a child, Zagreus. I was quite foolish with her from the start. Her father was a baseborn farm-boy; worshiped me! He's gone as well, of course. Only the cold remains.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: My daughter to a baseborn farm-boy, she... why, little sprout, I wasn't very kind to her, I think. Certainly not once her father passed. It was her mortal blood that made her weak! Born to mortals, taken by mortals, I suspect. So now, they pay.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't suppose... no, it is nothing, little sprout, it's just... I hear such wicked rumors now and then, about my daughter, lost to me, and what became of her. Kore, we called her, then. Persephone is what she called herself. Before she vanished utterly, leaving no trace....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: After my daughter Kore disappeared, I left this mountaintop. Wandered the earth, in search. For naught, of course. It was a necessary journey, I suppose. May yours fare better.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Is that the bloodlike stain of pomegranate on you, Zagreus? A hardy fruit resistant to a winter's chill. How fascinating they survive, down there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You bear the blessing of Poseidon, Zagreus? Someday I shall remind him of his place, and freeze his watery domain entirely.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: There's so much nonsense that the mortals say to one another of our family, Zagreus. How viciously the rumors spread! Whether from that oaf, Zeus, or likely from the mortal drunkard-poets with no better use of their limited time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: My sisters Hestia and Hera surely would assist as well, young sprout. Except they and our foster-brothers haven't been getting along of late. Though as for me? I'll never leave this mountaintop of mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: That chill sensation I detect must indicate that you are near the surface, Zagreus. If you can overcome the final challenges ahead, you soon shall see the long winter yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You live within the bosom of the earth, and know its warmth. But there is no such warmth up here, young Zagreus. You truly wish to feel the bitter cold of the outside? Then, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Someday, you'll gain the surface, Zagreus; and you shall see firsthand what mortalkind has wrought. You'll find it has some commonality with your domain.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I see the noble King of the Olympians has reached you prior to me, Zagreus. I wonder which of all his many lies he's told you last.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I ought to warn you that my foster-brother Zeus, he has just a disgusting sense of humor, little sprout. He'd have you think he sired half the countryside! I have to shudder at the very thought.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Last time I felt a chill like this was... on the surface, but, who...? Well, one way to find out. Then, in the name of Hades! Olympus, I accept this message!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I understand, young Zagreus, that you would seek to leave that bitter darkness for this bitter cold? Quite honestly I fail to see why. But I'll aid your plight, why not? For I can offer plenty of assistance, and you soon shall grow fond of it, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Good Lady Demeter, you've shown me warmth and generosity, and I am very grateful for it. Please, accept this as a small token of my appreciation.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: An offering well suited for the season, there, young Zagreus! In turn, as you are honorable and obedient, you'll have from me what the mortals have not.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: A little offering for you, good Lady Demeter. I'm deeply grateful for your willingness to aid me in all this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I must acknowledge and accept your thoughtful gift of Nectar, Zagreus; would that the mortals showed deference thus.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: May this small offering bring you some joy there on Olympus, Lady Demeter. Thank you for all your help.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: A conscientious gift, my little sprout; and I am grateful for your generosity. A bit of Nectar cuts straight through the cold, I say.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Your power and your wisdom has been of such help, good Lady Demeter. I'm honored, and this is the least I can do in exchange.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I was quite certain that the youth today was lacking any sort of tactfulness, young Zagreus. For once I'm thankful to be proven wrong.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Lady Demeter, I am forever in your debt for the affection that you've shown to me, and for the power you've bestowed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The everlasting winter means that Nectar's been in rather short supply; that you would think of me for such an offering is duly noted, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I often remember that there's sweetness in this world only by your grace, Lady Demeter. I'm thankful that we've grown closer through all this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You would make further offerings to me? Even though I have made quite clear I've lately not been at my charitable best, young Zagreus? You're either naive or you're much too kind, or both.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm, Grandmother? If you don't mind I call you that. Knowing we're related like this fills me with pride. Your power over life... I think I've some of it in me! And I could not have come this far without your help. Please, for everything... this is for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, Grandson, how you honor me, with this... I am so very grateful that we've met, and that... you're you, if that makes any sense at all. I have my sisters, but... I'd no one else, for very long, indeed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: That's Sister Hera's Heart-Seeker you have! The very weapon which she used to put our predecessors down. It hasn't lost its potency I see. Fear not, I shall not mention this to her, if I see her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, isn't that there Sister Hestia's oddly constructed armament you bear? She always was so predisposed to flame; I thought she'd never give it up! If it's to be in any hands, then may as well be yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I scarcely can see... are those the Twin Fists that you bear, young sprout? I once made ample use of them myself, if you'll imagine it. Wielding the cold since has been adequate for me, but you? May you put them to work as I once did.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I warn you, Zagreus, best not to make attempts to grow much closer with me, thus. I'm much your elder, after all, and you're my little sprout. What say we keep it that way, hm?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I... I am so very moved, I... have a daughter once again. And... I've a grandson, too! I thought, I felt something when first I met you, but... I brushed it off. Just like a fleck of snow...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't often divulge such blessings as I am about to, Zagreus. Don't thank me for it, but make certain that you use my power well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I have affection for you, little sprout. No need for reciprocity, but now I'll offer you the fiercest of my capabilities.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: My little grandson, little sprout, I've thought... if my Persephone's alive, why then... this winter, hasn't it endured for long enough...? It's grown to have a certain stubbornness, but I... I am the goddess of the seasons, Zagreus. Perhaps it's time I changed them once again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I've lifted the cold from the earth, much as I said I would, grandson. But, near your father's realm... I have decided it shall stay the way it is. Bereft of warmth. As a reminder to us all, about all this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Be thankful that the rivers to your realm have not yet frozen over, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I trust your ferry-boatman Charon has no qualms about the freezing temperatures up here?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: My message comes to you by river-boat this time, young Zagreus. At least that Charon is amenable to our attempts to contact you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, you've the look of one who has endured a terribly long winter, Zagreus! I trust you'll cling to life a little longer, now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I sense your grip on life is coming loose, young Zagreus. Now turn that cold you feel against your enemies.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You seem to be in dire need of some assistance, Zagreus. I think perhaps I have a bit to spare.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You're in the winter of your current stretch of life, aren't you, Zagreus? Do try and live a little longer for me, hm?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You waste away just like a common mortal, Zagreus. How rather unbecoming of a god.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The flame of life is easily extinguished by the cold, young Zagreus. Surely you feel the warmth within you giving way?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Cold comfort for your terrible predicament, young Zagreus, but I am here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps you'll exercise more wisdom in such interactions moving forward, hm?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: It ultimately isn't my intent to leave you in the cold like this, young Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The freezing cold ought to have drained the life from you; but I've allowed it to relent, for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Next time, young Zagreus, perhaps I'll take every last bit of warmth you've left down there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I am the eldest of Olympus, Zagreus. And I expect for you to treat me suitably.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The harsh of winter's gone for now, young Zagreus; and so take heart, but also you take heed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: So you survived a little winter's chill, didn't you, there? Be careful, though; next season could be worse.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Remember, next time, Zagreus, that on my whim I can take everything from you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I am quite capable of making your life plenty difficult, you do now realize, young Zagreus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps a few more winter seasons such as that might teach you more of how this world works.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Enough; I do not hold to grudges, Zagreus. You've my good graces, and my temper's cooled, for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I once had plentitudes of blessings I would offer readily, young Zagreus; but I am feeling not as generous of late.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Did you detect perhaps a certain spirit-shaking chill within the air, young Zagreus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Plenty of blessings left to go around; although, only for you, young Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Your enemies shall have no shelter from the cold whilst I am at your side, young Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: There's something to the barrenness, where you reside; perhaps someday it shall be more like that up here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Your realm there is much like the wind-swept surface here. Mostly devoid of life, such as it's been of late.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Olympus must seem like a distant paradise to you, young Zagreus; it is a frozen waste to me. But still, it's home.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Your foes shall more than catch cold by the time we're through, don't you agree, young sprout?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: All those who stand opposed to you, why, we shall shatter more than their resolve, I think, young Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: One of these seasons I suppose I ought to double-check the mortals haven't gone extinct without my intervention at each step.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: None can endure an everlasting winter's chill, young Zagreus; not even your father's lifeless servants there beneath the earth.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The seasons have a certain beauty to them, I suppose. Though struggle onward, and you needn't take my word for it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I've known your father and his brothers here for many seasons now; they bicker endlessly with me and one another.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I have to tell you something, little sprout. I take cold comfort knowing that my foster-brother Hades is no better at his parenting than I.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd gladly scour all your father's realm if doing so would free you from his clutches, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll have you know something, young Zagreus: The bitter cold and frost is just as inescapable as death itself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The seasons turn relentlessly, without regard, and thusly should you doggedly push forward on your path.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall bring desolation upon those who wrong you, little sprout.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I hope that you're aware how seldomly I give my blessing as of late, young Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: There's no escaping from the bitter cold, you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Your adversaries soon shall know something of loss, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Let us see to it your foes are left with nothing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: There shall be only souls for harvesting this day, I think, young Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: My warm regards, and all my blessings to you, little sprout.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The bitterness of cold and death go hand in hand, I do suppose.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: When all is taken away, the cold yet remains, young Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I am not feeling quite so generous of late, young Zagreus, but nevertheless: Choose.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Your lifeless foes must foolishly believe they've nothing left to lose.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Go with my blessings, Zagreus; though you may take but one.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Decay is the inevitable state of things; we are merely helping it along.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The cold of Mount Olympus shall bring utter ruin on your enemies.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Death and cold, they are irrevocably bound together, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The climate in those depths needs cooling, then, I take it, Zagreus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You bring with you the season of decay, young Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Your foes shall all wither before you like unwatered crops.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The frost eventually shall cover everything, young Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You'll find that life gives way quite easily to cold, young sprout.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Look carefully upon my blessings, Zagreus, and take no more than one.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I trust that harvesting dead souls is not unlike the harvest we have on the surface, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You're like a little flame there in your father's realm; easy enough to spot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: It seems to me your foes are ripe for harvesting, young Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Any who stand against you soon shall wither from the cold, young sprout.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, my little sprout, we shall have to get you through this winter's cold.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: A crisp and pleasant winter's evening to you, Zagreus; now, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: My finest winter blessings to you, little sprout.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, Grandson, it's growing rather cold, don't you agree?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm here to ease some of your burdens, my young sprout. That's what we grandmothers are for.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I trust you're keeping yourself warm enough in all of this, Grandson?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: When all this is over, please do come and visit us sometime, Grandson.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd tell you not to work too hard, Grandson, although your family is in need of more hard-working sorts.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Foul weather's on the forecast for your foes, Grandson.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't let my foster-brother bully you about, you hear, Grandson?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Let's cool down that hot head of yours a bit, why don't we, Grandson?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: My best regards to Queen Persephone and your lord father, little sprout. You keep it up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I trust your mother Queen Persephone is faring well if she has as fine a son as you, young Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Your mother sends you all her love from Mount Olympus, Grandson.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: All the best from Queen Persephone, young sprout. She misses you, you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Persephone is doing well here on Olympus, Zagreus. You ought to visit, too, sometime.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I seldom feel too generous of late, and yet for you, young Zagreus? Why not.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I once offered the mortals generosities as this in much more copious supply.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: It soon shall be exceptionally cold down in the Underworld, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd never give out blessings such as this to merely anyone, I hope you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I've quite the harvest here from which to choose, my little sprout.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Soon, the season there shall turn exceptionally cold, young Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: It is particularly cold outside, today; and soon it shall be so in there for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, little sprout, young Zagreus... my grandson! I can scarce believe it, still! To think that, Kore, or... Persephone, my daughter... she had been with Foster-Brother Hades, all this time! A match I wouldn't have expected... or approved. That's why she left! Our blasted hearts do what they want.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: By the way, we had entirely a lovely time within your father's house, Grandson. And, to see Kore once again, it... warmed my heart. I'm beyond grateful that your mother's safe. And that you've an important job to do. Here, I shall help.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I meant to tell you something, Grandson... you needn't have deceived the rest of us. You could have sent word that my daughter was with you down there, all along! Ah, but families are a complicated thing. Just swear that you'll be candid with us, next time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You must visit us sometime up here, Grandson! I understand the surface hasn't been agreeable with you... though perhaps if not in this age, then the next, we shall have you over. It's brisk up here... but nice enough, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Young sprout, I cannot fully understand my daughter's choices, such as what she saw in your gloom-filled father to have eloped with him. But if you are the result, then she is clearly wiser than I estimated.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I made mistakes raising your mother, sprout. When she was my Kore. I was blind to the possibility that she could have left me, but... I see it now. If you become a parent yourself, someday... may you listen more than I.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You tell my Kore... erm, Persephone, that is... the Queen! You tell her that her mother sends her warm regards. You tell her that. And you take care of her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Grandson, my daughter Queen Persephone, she sent me the most precious message, courtesy of Hermes, as of late. I'm pleased to hear that you are getting on all right. Though, I know you're at work, so here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I must admit I have grown fond of you, young sprout; nevertheless, I shall not spoil you like some ill-mannered child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: My, but you're as determined as my foster-brothers were, when my sisters and I stood with them 'gainst the Titans way back when.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: My blessing's seldom given and ought not be so readily refused, young Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I am accustomed to such disrespect from all those blasted mortals, Zagreus, but you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: If you are so disinterested in my generosity, young Zagreus, then you shall have my wrath.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't recall if I yet warned against this type of attitude, although it doesn't matter either way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Do you believe I give my blessings liberally, Zagreus? Well then I've news for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Offend me, and I'll drain the last traces of color from those cheeks of yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: That was an ill-advised decision, Zagreus. I'm past quickly forgiving such mistakes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You truly think you can withstand the ravages of winter, Zagreus? That I would like to see.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: A harsh winter is coming for you, Zagreus. And I'm afraid you've brought it on yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: For that decision, Zagreus, it is about to be a very cold day down there in your Underworld.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: A careless choice you've made, there, Zagreus. As you grow long in years, perhaps you shall learn better judgment as to whom to trust, and whom to never, ever disrespect.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Someday, my little sprout, you'll gain the surface of your father's realm, and see my handiwork. Until such time, I'll come to your aid as I can. It's quite the entertaining pastime, honestly!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Remember something for us, Zagreus. A frozen heart holds no sway over anyone at all. Isn't that right, oh Aphrodite, dear?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Aphrodite_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I don't know, my dearest Demeter! The frozen heart has tendency to thaw, and then begin to beat with renewed vigor after that. I trust your time shall come! It can never be too late, except for mortals, the poor things.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Young Zagreus, once you arrive, do tell Lord Ares here that his great power's not worth anything without my own. Wars have their causes, among which, many I control.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Ares_01
dialogueLines.text: I do not question that your influence can stir a mortal's impulses toward war, O Goddess Demeter. At least we can agree that our domains are quite compatible!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, Zagreus, my gentle Artemis here was just saying how concerned she was for your predicament! I'm sure she'd like it if we both could help you in this case.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Concerned? I don't see anyone concerned, you have this, Zagreus! You don't need anybody's help, and you can get through this all on your own, though just in case, and I mean just in case! Um, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: To think, young sprout, our strategist Athena, warrior-goddess, sprang from Zeus' head! I would expect that no high intellect could come from there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Please do not mind the Lady Demeter's good-natured sense of humor, Zagreus. She and my father often like to jest with one another, even in the most difficult times.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The crops shall wither and decay so long as I may please, young Zagreus. Although we do have those upon Olympus who take issue with my reasoning...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Erm, yeah, milady, I take issue here! I mean how are the grapes supposed to grow and wine supposed to flow when you are so uptight, now, just ease up a little there, let's see what we can do!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, little sprout. I was just notifying Lord Poseidon here that all of his domain exists because it pleases me; remember, my good foster-brother, I could freeze your oceans solid if I chose.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Poseidon_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, hahahah! Excellently stated, venerable Goddess Demeter! I'm reminded once more of your vast experience, though, I think we all prefer our oceans liquidy, isn't that right, Nephew?!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, now, young sprout, you've met the so-called King of the Olympians, and I. Whom do you now suppose is genuinely ruler of this snow-encrusted mountaintop of ours?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zeus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, hah-hah-hah, Lady Demeter! Your wit is just as dry as the earth lying quite fallow in your care! Perhaps you should go tend to that again, though let's we go ahead and help our young man first!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Say, man, if I'm not mistaken, I think you got yourself a bottle of Ambrosia, there? I'd no idea you could get that stuff downstairs! Do me a solid, though, hang on to any more you find, or better yet, give it away! I'd hate to think even a drop has gone to waste!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, would you just look at yourself, Zag! You are like a walking stockpile of Ambrosia, aren't you? Taking the best care of it, at that. I knew I could count on you, but... <Sniff> I didn't know I could be just so proud of you, you know, man?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Zag, turns out I am quite the judge of character, and you, man, have an awful lot of it, I mean, you've given out how much Ambrosia now? To all your mates, and more?!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: You got to let me in on something, Zag, how did you gather up so much Ambrosia, there? I mean, sometimes I think the stuff is plentiful up here, but you are positively swimming in it, man!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Whoa, hey, you must have had a run-in with the lovely Aphrodite, am I right, or am I right, there Zag? But I have just the thing to go with her!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: You hanging out with Ares, are you, Zag? He's quite the chap, I'll give him that, I'll give him that, and now, I'll give you this!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Zag, got to tell you, I cannot believe that little Artemis is hanging out with you! She doesn't hang with anybody, man! But I digress, take this!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey man, I heard Athena had a word with you already yeah? She's cool, though, once you get to know her. Here! I got something that ought to suit her style.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Felt a shiver in the air just now, and that can only mean that chilly Demeter decided to give you a little help, there, huh? Or maybe it's just cold, I mean, this is a mountaintop!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: You are the talk of the mountain, you know that, man? Good on you, getting on just about everybody's good side up here, I mean, that is a feat, all right!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Zag, uh... I may just be losing it or something here, but isn't that your dad's own blessing that you've got? That's something, man, I didn't know that he was helping you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Say, isn't that the Overflowing Cup I handed down to you, I'm glad you like it, man! I guess it's overflowing still, because I figure otherwise I couldn't tell at all!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Whoa, Zag, heard you're hanging with the Fury Megaera of late, well cheers to that, man, bet she's something else, but you just watch for that whip of hers, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey man, I'm hearing up here that you got in good with Megaera, the Fury, is that right, I mean, I hope it is, because, hahaha, yeow, know what I mean? They say she's something else!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: You, man, must be mates with just about everybody down there, by now, yeah? Doling out Nectars left and right, just livening the place right up!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Say, let me just point out you've got a lot of Nectar on you, man! Oh, I can tell, I like the stuff a lot, myself, but even I don't pack that much, and I mean, wow!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hope this lives up to your expectations, mate. In the name of Hades! Olympus? This is an official message! Should be a good time, Lord Dionysus!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Whoa, hold just for a moment, Zag, now, I can recognize an invite when I see one, man! And this is definitely almost certainly some sort of invite, one for me, and... one for everybody else, it looks like, here? Hoh, I'm excited, that is something really special, I can tell!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey Zag, ever see a fellow by the name of Orpheus down there, you ever heard of him? I bet you have, and I have had a funny thought, a little jest that maybe we could try, if you'd be up to have a little harmless fun?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Harmless fun at the expense of Orpheus? You have my full and complete attention, Dionysus mate.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...That chap comes up with the most smashing songs, so I was thinking, maybe we could spin him a tall tale, something like how maybe you and I, like, we're connected or something? He'll buy it, tell him, tell him for me, yeah?!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hahaha, Zag! I heard you got to Orpheus, he's got this whole entire ballad now, about how you are really me, or maybe it's the other way around, I've no idea, hahahaha! Here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hoh-ho, no way Zag, looking like you got yourself a gift from old Poseidon there? He's quite the catch, but here, hope you saved some room for more!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Zag, I heard that Orpheus, he found his missing bride, why, isn't that the sweetest thing, isn't it, man? I'll drink to that, and I think so should you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, you're in good with Thanatos, isn't that right? A real broody sort, sometimes I think maybe I ought to just invite him to a feast, but then, what if he cools the whole thing down, makes everybody kind of ehhhhh, you know?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, you've a Yarn of Ariadne, right there, Zag, I know the lass for whom that yarn is named! She was a right attractive one, we had our little time, long story, though, will save it till you get here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: So Zag, you met my dad by now, your uncle Zeus? He's cool and loves a feasting, just... be sure to keep him in your thoughts. OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sometimes I have to wonder, man, what's going through your head, I mean, I know you hear me and the others but it's so damn dark down there I can't hear you at all!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Let me tell you something, Zag, I know your dad he's got to be a real handful, I relate to that, you understand me, mate? So you get out of there, and you'll be more than welcome here, with us!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: That was... revelry? People having a good time? Well, sure, then! In the name of Hades! Olympus, I'll accept this message.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Heeey, there, Zag, man, how's it going? Look, you have got to get here with the rest of us already, we've been saving you a spot! Let me see what I can do, make life a little sweeter for you in the meantime!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Lord Dionysus, it's been great getting to know you, so, please accept this humble offering.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Now that is something, a right princely gift, there, Zag, and just so happens I got something here I have been meaning to give you!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: A toast in your honor, Lord Dionysus mate. Have I mentioned how pleased I am to have made your acquaintance?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hoh-oh, what, is that for me, really, I mean, cheers man, that is awful generous of you, I'm much obliged!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Lord Dionysus mate, you have made the mood down here materially less miserable, and I am much obliged.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: What, is that for me, why Zag, I mean you really shouldn't have, you know I can't get enough of this stuff, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Lord Dionysus mate, you must have no shortage of supreme vintage at your disposal, but this one's from me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Why Zag, how positively thoughtful, man, I mean I love it, positively love this stuff, and I am very moved that you would think of me for it!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, on no occasion whatsoever, cheers to you again, Lord Dionysus mate! This place is so much less dreary under your influence!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, this is the greatest, Zag! This feast I'm headed to? I was getting a little concerned I wasn't going to have quite enough to go around for everyone. Well, you just saved my evening, man! Too bad you couldn't be there, yeah?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Dionysus mate, sometimes things weigh heavily on me, but then I hear from you, and it's like I don't have a care in the world! To your health!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you're really too much for me sometimes, you know that, man? Got to tell you, I don't think I ever felt so close to somebody so damn far away. But through the gift of drink? It's like we're with each other in spirit, all the time, you know?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What does one offer to the god of wine? The very best, is what, and down here, this is it, mate! I'm grateful I've got some of your spirit in me. To have such a connection, with you despite all the distance between us...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Zag, I cannot believe what you are offering to me! Ambrosia, man, for me? And talk about a bottle that has seen some wild days and nights, I mean if it got all the way to you down there, only to come back up! Know what? I'm saving it, till we can share it, here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: I've got a feeling this time, man, like things are really going to take a turn for you, and I mean for the better, almost certainly, I think!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: The key to any halfway decent feast is lots and lots of preparation, man. Or maybe just having to improvise, you know?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh-hoh, Zag, have I got something special here for you, I mean this here is top-notch stuff, even among the top-notch stuff I have to give, just look at this!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Look, man, I am feeling generous right now, know what I mean? And so, here's something extra potent for you, ought to keep you going for a while longer than you're used to, there!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Heeey, what's, uh, with that Charon, man, he's sort of, I-don't-know, though I am glad he's been of service!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah-hah, you found me holing up in Charon's nasty stash there did you man, well good, good, here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Looks like you are stocked for one hell of a night down there, man! I knew Charon would hook you up with what you need!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, cheers for picking up the tab, there, Zag, now, I cannot exactly pay you back, though I could give you this!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: I bet that Charon has to be the life of every feast you have down there, with such a personality, am I not right about that, man?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Whoaa Zag, man, you look positively knackered, you be sure and take it easy if you can out there, all right? Here, maybe this'll help!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oof, Zag, look, I know you're not in a good spot right now, you must be really out of sorts! Though maybe this might numb the pain a bit!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Eugh, you're a proper mess right now, got to be candid with you, man, you're bleeding everywhere, get it together, yeah?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Now what's the matter, down there, Zag, it's like you've been up feasting day and night, you're barely standing, everything OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ooh, you're in no shape to be going off, what happened to you, there? Well, here, you know what? Let me see if we can get you back into it, man!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: All right, all right, all right, we're cool, we're cool, no need to get all hot and bothered any longer, here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: OK, let's say we're settled up now, Zag, and go on being best mates just like before, yeah?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, Zag, you are not so bad, just had a little lapse in judgment, happens to me all the time, now let's get on with it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, now, that was quite a sight, I got to hand it to you, man, and you deserve this, here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Know what, man, I'm not even angry after that. What was I even going off about, do you recall...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: OK, all right, it's fine already, Zag, you win this time and I am good as grapes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Appreciate your understanding, there, man, so let's just avoid this type of little shake-up next time, yeah?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hahahahahah, OK, thanks for a good laugh, there, Zag, what was it I was going on about, before...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I just can't stay mad about you, Zag, and to be honest, why, I wasn't that mad to begin with, I just thought I'd shake your branches up a bit!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're all right, man, you're all right, don't even worry about the rough patch we just crossed back there, we're stronger than all that!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: I figured this time I'd stay mad at you for sure, man, but you know what, I just couldn't! No matter how hard I tried.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Know what, man, just forget about what happened, yeah? 'Cause I already did, I mean, it happens, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Looks like this feast is getting started still, am I not right about that, Zag? Come on, let's show everybody a good time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Just getting warmed up down there, yeah, Zag? Here, I've a fine selection of my blessings, just for you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Whooaa, Zag, I bet you could use a little pick-me-up right about now, and I am here to offer you whichever one you please!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey Zag, how is life, you hanging in there, yeah? I reckoned maybe we could raise a glass here, to your health, but then I thought, 'No, wait! I can do a little more than that!' So, here we go!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, how you living life down there right now, all right I take it, yeah? No, wait, though, I have just the thing!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, hello, Zag! Fancy running into you, though there's no need to work yourself so hard, you hear me, yeah? I reckon this should take the edge right off!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: How goes it this time, man, everything fine? Though hold that thought, I wager we can make things finer yet!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: That Underworld of yours, place is a real pit, isn't it Zag, a shame you're stuck down there. But we are going to get you out of there, together, you and I!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Soon as you get up to Olympus, man... it's going to be a feast like you have never seen, you mark my words! Now take this, and good luck!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: You give those musty shades and corpses a fair fighting chance down there, you hear me, Zag? Though just in case you need a hand...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: You got this, man! I so believe in you, it's almost like the Greeks believe in me! And they believe in me an awful lot, you know!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Time for another go at getting out of there, eh, Zag? Well not to worry, we'll make something happen, let's hop to it then!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Now listen up here, Zag, 'cause this time I have got something for you that's going to put some extra fire in your step, you ready, man?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: You wait for just a moment, Zag, 'cause I got just the thing for this occasion, you are going to love it, get a load of this!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Now, Zagreus, I'm certain everything is going fine for you right now, but just in case, but just in case! I got a little something, just for you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's me, Zag, hey, I know you haven't got a lot of time to listen to me prattle on like this, and so, I'm cutting to the chase here, look!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey again as ever Zag, now you keep at it like you have been and I bet we'll all be hanging out up here in no time flat! I'm with you all the way!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, hey, let's see, man, I am thinking that this time around this smattering of my concoctions ought to do you right!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Here's to an interesting time, how about that, yeah?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd like to make a toast, to you of course my friend! Good luck out there!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, hey, have I got just the thing for this particular occasion, man!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Zag, what is going on, you going to get out of here this time? Why, yes, I think you are!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: OK Zag. You and me. We're getting through this, now or never, ready, yeah?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh hey, Zag, here's a toast to your success, so... clink! Cheers and all that!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Here's something for you man, grab it and go!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: I got you, Zag, got just the thing, right here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah there you are, man, I got what you need!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: I know you're busy, man, so here you go, get on with it, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's good to kind of see you down there man, now here, take this!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure is dark there, Zag, I hope you're hanging in there, yeah?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: You getting anywhere, yet, man? 'Cause if you're not, it's cool, we've all got time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Got something that is going to knock you off your feet, here, man!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: You ready for this, Zag, now here's a good one, courtesy of me!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's going to be a real feast here once you make it, man!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Only the best for you, man, now check this out, you'll see!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: This here, Zag, is among the best I have to offer, it is good!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm feeling really good right now, and thought I'd pass that right along!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Here's something extra special for you, man, now cheers!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Past time we got things going for you there, what do you say, you ready for this, man?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: I got to make it to a feast in just a little bit, here, man, but quickly, take this, yeah?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: I know it's been tough times of late, but look, you just keep going, and you'll make it, man!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: This time for sure, I mean, I know you're going to make it to the top, together with me, man!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey I propose a toast to you, there, man, and may you always kill whatever's in your path, and on and on!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: A toast, man, to your health! Or to the lack of it, for anybody who gets on your bad side, yeah?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Have I got just the thing for you, here, man, you ready? Here we go!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: What do you say we go all out this time, how about it, Zag, you with me, man, or what?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Been saving something for the right occasion, though I figure it's as good a time as any, yeah?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, cheers to you, and everything you do, there, Zag! Only the best from me to you, man!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Look, Zag, I don't entirely recall what happened at that feast you threw for all of us down there, but let me tell you, I am under the impression it went smashingly! It was amazing, man! Let's have another one sometime, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, back when we had that feast, there, man, I, I hope I didn't, you know, make for any inconveniences or anything down there, did I...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Zag, I cannot believe your mom's Persephone, that's something else, I got to tell you, man! I never took her for the sort to be all into Underworld stuff!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Now how's my favorite Underworld god surviving, Zag, you holding up or what? Right better momentarily, I bet!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Man oh man, Zag, someday you will be the guest of honor at the biggest feast that you have ever seen! Though I know you have got your hands full for the time, so, first things first!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, come on, Zag, and here I thought that we were cool. You hurt my pride, there, man. I guess I'm going to have to hurt yours....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ohhh man, that was definitely not a thing to do, I mean, are we best mates, or not...? You think on that, while I do this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Look Zag, I'm not going to lie, you hurt my feelings here, I thought we had a thing! A close familial bond! I'm really mad now that I think on it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: What, you wouldn't hang with me given the choice? I thought I had you sorted, man, but maybe you just don't appreciate the finer things!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, come on, Zag, now that was not the choice I would have made, you messing with me, man? All right! I'll mess with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ohh, we all make mistakes from time to time, man, and you ought to know! You made one there just now!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Why do it to me like that, Zag, man, I thought we were close as grapes upon the vine, but I was wrong?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Did I do something to deserve this, man, because I thought that we were getting on just fine, better than fine, but this?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's not that I'm upset or anything, here, Zag, you know I'm not, but truthfully I am a bit annoyed, a little bit, and so, I got to send the message, man.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Really, man? After everything we've been through together, this is the gratitude I get?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, way to spoil the feast, there, Zag, I mean come on, I thought I was your mate? Was I mistaken, man?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Zag, I can take a jest, ask anybody, but when it comes to competition here, it's not a laughing matter! Do you understand me, man?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, Zag, what happened last time, man? It seemed like you were rather close, but then, we couldn't see you anymore from here, and now you're back? Well, give another go!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Want you to know something, here, man. It's been a bit of a long haul, but you are getting closer to the surface, and you'll get there someday, and we're with you, however long it takes, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: I got to hand it to you, Zag, you don't back down, you don't ever back down, what is this, like the twentieth time that you've attempted to escape?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're getting somewhere, getting somewhere, man, I understand you made it up to Asphodel before, now that is progress, keep it up, you keep on going, yeah?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Heard you wrestled with some Satyrs near the surface, Zag! Nice work making it that far, and I just want to let you know that they are definitely not my crew, man! Satyrs I know aren't like that at all!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Erm, hey, man, you been having some bad run-ins with that Fury, Megaera, is that her name? Now isn't she a real firebrand! Maybe this'll help you out next time you meet!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Heard that you took care of Theseus down in Elysium of late, there, Zag! Nice work, never liked that one, between us. There's this nice lass helped him out one time, and he just dumped her rotten, I don't like that, man!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Look, Zag, you should know by now that when it comes to feasting, it's Yours Truly here who puts on the best performance. But I do have Lady Aphrodite's help!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Aphrodite_01
dialogueLines.text: Wine does have a rather special way of making everybody look even more beautiful than ever, Dionysus dear! And from such beauty, why, long-lasting love is born! And also shorter-lasting love, much of the time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, Zag, maybe once you get here, you could get old Ares to just lighten up a little, you know, man? Always dragging down the mood, with talk of blood, and nastiness, all sorts of stuff like that!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Ares_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, my Lord Dionysus! I fail to see why such discussion is perceived as unbecoming of a feast. A successfully conducted war is certainly a cause for celebration, is it not?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Zag, man, I was just attempting here to get our good friend Artemis to finally make an appearance at one of my feasts, it's just... wouldn't you know, she's all preoccupied, something about a hunt?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: That's right, Lord Dionysus, a hunt! Which requires lots and lots of total quiet, and clear-minded focus. So, if you'll excuse me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Zag, when you arrive, it's going to be a real scene, I'll tell you now! Even Athena here will cut loose for a bit, let down her hair, you know, put on something more comfy, yeah, it's going to be great!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Athena_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps Lord Dionysus here is the true god of strategy, as it appears he is uncannily aware of my next move, Cousin. Although, be that as it may, I shall attempt to remain focused on your situation. Not his feast.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey Zag, got a favor here I mean to ask, could you maybe persuade the Lady Demeter to ease up on the whole 'not let anything grow' thing she's been going on about?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I daresay, my Lord Dionysus, that partaking of less wine for some amount of time might do you good. Regardless, you'll not coax a better crop from me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Uh, Zag, erm... your uncle's passing through. Not Dad, erm, the other one I mean, and he just wanted to check in, see how you're doing, so...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Poseidon_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah-hah, there you are, my little nephew! Dionysus showing you the ropes, or what? You want a real feast, though, you just stick with me! Now take my blessing, go!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, anyway we're here, man, waiting up for you! We know you're going to make it, I would say eventually?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dionysus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey Zag, my Dad, he's here and wants to have a word, be cool man, go along with what he says, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zeus_01
dialogueLines.text: That Dionysus, always much too kind with introductions, though at least he shows me suitable respect. Perhaps someday he can instruct you in the customs of Olympus here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: Aphrodite's really something, don't you think so, Coz? Normally I'd stop for no one here. But when it comes to her, exceptions must be made!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: You know who keeps me awful busy, Coz? Ares. Nothing like a good old war amongst the mortals to create demand for lots and lots of messages to go around!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: Did Artemis manage to locate you already, boss? She's pretty good at that, I have to say. I'm faster! But she's fast, she's pretty fast, all right.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: So then Athena's looking out for you, that's good, that's good! She likes to put a lot of thought and stuff in everything, it's all a little much, if you ask me!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: Somebody's on a tight deadline, I take it, Coz? Courtesy of your father's Pact, well, you believe me, I know how that is, so I won't keep you, just keep moving, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: I realize you're on a tight deadline, there, boss, so let's dispense with all unnecessary chit-chat here and get you on your merry way, posthaste!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: You racing against time to keep things interesting, or what, there, boss? Turns out that time goes pretty quick. I like racing against it, too!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: I can't believe you got into a fight with my professional associate, you daft or something, Coz? Just about everybody that I know, they're all at least a little bit afraid of that one and his boat, yet you think you can take him, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: So you took on my good professional associate, is that right, Coz? And you beat the big boatman himself! I knew you were pretty tough, but not that tough! Impressive work! He's impressed, too. He isn't even mad!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: Been downright chilly up here lately, Coz! We've Demeter to thank for that! Me, I try to stay out of her way, she doesn't think I'm of much help to her. Although I'm sure she's warmed to you at least!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: You ever get the feeling life's been moving all too quickly for you, boss? Well Dionysus doesn't, as you're probably aware. Throws quite a feast, though!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: Got all your father's lifeless wretches hot and bothered, going all out all because of that Forced Overtime condition of his, isn't that right, Coz? Well, if they're interested in getting things done quickly, I am sure you can oblige them in a bit!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: Looks like those lifeless wretches you're contending with down there are really on the move this time around, aren't they, boss? Oh, well, you're faster still, I'm sure!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey boss, in all your travels there, you happen to run into a lass by name Eurydice? Hope she's all right! I feel bad because we didn't get a proper send-off last we met.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: I caught wind you found Eurydice down there, that's great! Long as she's on the up and up, I can eject her from the tiny worry center of my mind.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: Met your father face to face only the one time, Coz. Despite rendering services to him for quite a while. Memorable experience, I'll give it that! Seems like he's not done anything with all the messages my side keeps sending him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: Say, isn't that your father's blessing on you, Coz?! I almost can't believe it, but you've done such a good job of things, how could he not approve?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: Thought I'd check up on you, there, boss. You doing well, everything good and all? Can't hear you one iota, mind, so then, don't answer that!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: That's quite a nice Bone Hourglass you got, there, boss! So you're in good with my professional associate, as well. A real gentleman!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: Come quickly, boss! You need to hurry up and get out of there, you're running out of time!! You're not, really, but it's a bit of motivation, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: Heard you've been seeing the Fury, Megaera, Coz? Got the impression she was quite a handful, though I guess the same can probably be said of you, there, huh? You two have fun!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I get to be the messenger this time. In the name of Hades! Olympus, this is an official message! Lord Hermes? Got here as quickly as I could.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, that looks like you've got a message just for me, this time, how special is that, boss? Some sort of invite from the looks of things. You sure that I'm supposed to have this, seeing as I'm not officially involved?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: I hear you're close with Orpheus down there, you get the chance, you tell him I said hey! He's pretty decent with that lyre of his, last time I checked.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: Heard that you finally got out of there, Coz! Big congratulations due for that: Congrats! The secret's safe with me, of course! The others here, they think you're still at it. Though, I guess you are!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: Your mother got from there just fine, don't worry, Coz! She had me looking after her, and such. Seemed real nice! Told me to tell you hi.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, I never knew the pomegranate seeds down in the Underworld held such power if not for your mother mentioning it, Coz! And here I thought they were just tasty, that was it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, look at you, there, boss! I recognize that fancy plume you got! Just an iota of my speediness in there, but still! Much more than most can do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey looking like you're getting some good use out of that fancy plume of yours! Keep going like that, maybe someday you'll be half as quick as me!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: Want to bring you in on something, boss. Reason that Uncle Poseidon got to you before I did is, I let him. He's under the impression he's as spry as ever! Hah!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: So, now you've found me out, haven't you, Coz? Heard from the big boat-man himself. Well, good! We're pleased to be of service. And we've got a lot of dirt on you, so you be sure to keep our secrets, too. All right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: So my professional associate, the fearsome boatman on the River Styx? You got to know him pretty well in all of this, right, Coz? Hard getting through to him, I know, but his heart's in the right place! It's why I'm working with him, after all this time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
dialogueLines.text: I got a keen eye, boss, so I happened to notice that you've got a certain tooth you're carrying about. Know you're from the land of the dead and all, but don't you think that comes across as just a little odd?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hermes_01
